label: neither
rationale: Criticism directed at a named individual (Sifuna) framed as a rhetorical question about political strategy; 'Luhya's' appears as an electoral-bloc reference, not as a target of contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'The only people who you can buy are luhyas and luos' attributes collective corruptibility to two ethnic communities as an immutable trait - NCIC collective contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Luo GOONS' combined with 'uprooting railway lines and burning tyres' attributes collective criminal violence to the Luo community, transferring individual acts onto the group with contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'the most useless and confused community that ever existed' and 'professional watchmen, shamba boys na maids' collectively degrade the entire Luhya community - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wewe ni mluhya mjinga' ties the individual insult to an ethnic marker, then 'your bloodline is of witches and night runners' extends degradation collectively to the lineage.

label: hate
rationale: 'wajinga wengine watano' ('other five idiots') collectively attributes stupidity to Luhya supporters as a group defined by ethnicity alongside named figure Sifuna.

label: hate
rationale: 'foolish luhyas', 'hii wivu ya waluhya ni real' and 'senile oburu' collectively attribute jealousy and foolishness to the Luhya community as a whole - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: offensive
rationale: 'Filthy corrupt bootlicking gakunia' is crude personal abuse aimed at one named individual (Anne Waiguru), not transferred onto any ethnic community.

label: hate
rationale: 'You are the fools' addressed collectively to 'my Murima people' attributes collective foolishness to the Mt Kenya ethnic community.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus are dumb', 'a community still can't think', 'Motherfuckers' - collective attribution of stupidity and gullibility to the entire Kikuyu community - plain NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Jaluo mjinga' ties an ethnic slur directly to an insult, then 'nyie Ni goons' collectively attributes criminal thug behaviour to the Luo community.

label: hate
rationale: 'have never seen a stupid community like luos' is a direct, unambiguous collective attribution of stupidity to the Luo community - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Waislamu hawataki kheir ya dini yao' ('Muslims don't want the good of their religion') collectively attributes religious indifference or failure to the Muslim community in Malindi.

label: neither
rationale: Claims Gachagua (a named politician) brought 'goons' affiliated with Kisii; criticism aimed at the politician's conduct, not a collective attack on the Kisii community.

label: hate
rationale: 'The Kissii organise chinkororo to kill their own children' holds the entire Kisii community collectively responsible for criminal acts - NCIC group-blame standard.

label: hate
rationale: 'economic activity ya LUOS ni VITA and goonism' and 'just idiots' collectively attribute violence, criminality and stupidity to the entire Luo community - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'nilotes are just bad at leadership' collectively attributes an inherent leadership deficiency to the entire Nilotic ethnic grouping - NCIC collective contempt, even though mildly phrased.

label: hate
rationale: 'Wamunyoro luos are scum' is a direct collective dehumanising slur applied to the Luo community - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: neither
rationale: Accusation that a named individual (Alionya) is a government mole; personal political accusation with no protected-characteristic targeting.

label: neither
rationale: Generic rebuke of 'Kenyans' as lazy and cheating; nationality is not a protected characteristic under the NCIC ethnic-hatred standard and no ethnic community is targeted.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus are the most tribal lot in kenya' collectively attributes tribalism as an immutable trait to the entire Kikuyu community - NCIC ethnic contempt.

label: hate
rationale: 'Kikuyus organized violence against Muslims', 'organized Post Election Violence against Luos, Luhyas and Kisiis' holds the entire Kikuyu community collectively responsible for mass violence, and 'the goons are back' implies renewed threat.

label: hate
rationale: Quoted speech 'ODM ni ya wajaluo na goons peke yake' equates the Luo community with criminal goons; the post reproduces rather than condemns the framing, and no distancing stance is present.

label: hate
rationale: 'poor kikuyus are willing to do [anything] just to keep a wicked oath alive' attributes collective moral corruption and oath-bound deviance to the Kikuyu community as a whole.

label: neither
rationale: Challenges Kikuyus on a historical political choice (supporting 'the killer') framed as political criticism and a call to learn; harsh but the target is a named action/leader and a political decision, not collective ethnic contempt.
